reviews.review_description: After reading other reviews I was a little bit hmmmm....shall I or not... and then I thought 3 quid I spend more on a k.f.c that lasts 5 mins so i did I paid for the game and downloaded it.... I can honestly say yes what a buy. Ok yes it does make you feel a bit nautious BUT I changed the view to behind the rider and it went so i went back to in helmet view. This i can honestly say has had me smiling constantly whikst playing it, the only downfall is my phone over heats and i have to stop playing grrr.. for 3 quid this game is fun the graphics are in my opinion miles ahead of a lot of others games iv seen on vr and if your not in it just for fun then maybe get a goldfish  because people expect to much and this game is fun and in my opinion for the price you cant go wrong. I love the fact your on the grid with a lot of other bikes around and the details. I take my hat off and say well done and thanks for the fun.

It does not work with any standard gamepad/controller! Which means it does NOT work with the motion controller, NOR by headset point-direction... At all!!!!!
The error is: "there is no controller connected, or the controller disconnected", something like that, can't remember exactly.

It does work with the Samsung gamepad/controller, though...... Congratulations Developer, you made a game that works with only 1 device, that was "discontinued" 10 years ago.............

Either fix this cr-app, or refund us and take it down! Thank you for your understanding.

Hi! I'm Dreaded Kane, developer of another motorcycle racing game on Gear VR called Incline.

Thank you for releasing this on Gear VR, I bought it immediately! I'm a huge fan of motorcycle games and have all the SBK and MotoGP games on PC from SBK 08 onwards.

There is a strange disconnect between the controller and the motorcycle when leaning  I'm not sure exactly what the reason is but I've tried the mobile versions of your game a few times and couldn't get into them for this reason. I use a linear 1:1 controller mapping in my game and it seems to work well.

The camera is tilted slightly downwards which threw off my balance. You should try to keep the horizon level at all times to reduce motion sickness. Using head tracking to steer is another good method. I had to stop after three laps because I felt too ill to continue.

It looks very promising and if you get these issues fixed I can see myself spending many hours in-game. All the best!

developer_response.body: Hi, Dreaded Kane! Thank you so much for your feedback and suggestions, they're much appreciated. Motion sickness and control are major issues in VR racing games and we're still beginners, but we'll do our best to improve. So far we've introduced the helmet camera and partially blocked the horizon tilt to prevent nausea, but mobile controllers seem to have a very lousy response compared to console controllers and mapping racing games on them is far from easy. Let's keep in touch for any collaboration opportunities: you can reach us here http://www.dtales.it/gaming/en/contact-us.php

reviews.review_description:
update..  looks so good... shame it feels so unplayable....just need the controls sorted.

Is it not possible to give us some sort of tunning options... for steering at least...and manual gears you cant control a bike in automatic.... 

this could be a great title on Gearvr...but it isn't.......sorry

----------------

Being a fan of the racing sim genre. I was surprised to see this.... and there is no doubt it looks great. 

Control feels a little weird. And there is no option for manual gear change...sound is awful. Auto brake option cant be switched off. ..... You can switch it off but it stays on.....
 
Devs... have a go at  "Incline"on gearvr. Get the controls sorted and this is definitely something i will play. 

Homewreckers studios Incline. feels more immersive. Though its not as pretty.

G
Can you make the safe games synch with the standard Android game?
Great game love playing this on the train while it's moving, Moga hero controllers work perfectly...
Pretty good racing title for the Gear VR. I use a cheap Moga gamepad Still learning the courses but having fun.
Finally a super fun and challenging VR motorbike racing game. Graphics is beautiful and smooth. Great control. Love it.

I think it's a shame that this title doesn't get exposure outside of the Gallery section because I'm quite sure that there are a lot of people who might like it but don't know that it exists.

It's something of an under-appreciated gem I think.
want let me play with out a contoler when says you can play on touch pad

developer_response.body: Thank you for reporting this issue. As a matter of fact, you’re right: the game details on the store indicate the Touchpad as a supported device, because you can navigate menus with it, but you actually need a Gamepad to play the racing sessions. Although we have indicated both the required (Gamepad) and supported (Touchpad and Gamepad) devices correctly on our developers’ dashboard, the info provided on the game store page may be misleading and you’re not the only user reporting it. We have contacted the Oculus support team to fix this issue but the only thing you can do, if you don’t have a Gamepad, is to ask for a refund of your purchase (https://tickets.oculus.com/hc/en-us/requests/new). Please, accept our sincerest apologies for the inconvenience. We’ll do our best to avoid this from happening again.

reviews.review_description: This game is not supporting my Samsung oculus controller.
if i could i give you 4stars and half but to be with nick hayden i give you the 5 stars RIP Mr Hayden

Good game, despite what people wrote you can fall off and you can turn off auto braking. Audio is weak, needs some polish in the animation department . Control's are good . I have a feeling in a few months when it's worked on it will go from good to great.
Nothing too crazy here, as good a graphics and physics as can be expected for a phone VR game. Makes me keen to see a full on VR bike game on PC or console at some point.

Very immersive, it looks like you are on a motorbike. Still not perfect though, I wish it could support the Oculus go controller (to be used as throttle+brake) and the use of the bending of the head to make curves. Then it would reach a new level of realism and probably become the best game for Oculus.
Fun racer... controls not too bad
Great game for the money. You have to put lots of practise in to get good in the hard mode.

reviews.review_title: Gear vr controller can not be used
reviews.review_description: Developer update game please! Gear vr controller can not be used .i pay money to buy but can not play the game........

developer_response.body: We're sorry for the inconvenience but the Gear VR Controller is currently not supported. As indicated on the game's store page, SBK VR requires a gamepad to play. We may support the Gear VR Controller in the near future though.

reviews.review_description:
First you need a gamepad to play the go controller does not work graphics and playability are great for the go.  There are different track. events ;16 detailed circuits. and 24 different riders to chose from    
there are different views and for the newbies assists in controls. I.e.braking and steering. Worth every penny to buy
Good game. However, needs more responsive brakes!
